Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for BrightLoop correspondence using search_emails (query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or similar). Discover Tessa's Series B expansion email requesting six May relocations (Oliver Bancroft from London, Priya Naidu with ADA service dog, Jonathan Marsh executive white-glove, Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes), Simone Richter's April 8 complaint about being placed in a studio instead of a promised 1BR, Mina's unanswered April 9 follow-up, Road Runner dispatch delay notice (April 11) to blessing.okafor@moveops.com for Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic, Marcus Webb's two unanswered follow-ups to support@moveops.com, Mina's escalation email to Chloe and David, Chloe's ops gaps email, Alejandro's and Marcus Thorne's pricing analysis emails (standard domestic quotable but Oliver Bancroft international and Jonathan Marsh executive lack formalized pricing), Fatimah's email flagging Oliver Bancroft as a true international case with visa/customs unknowns, and Mina's holding reply to Tessa.
- Search Slack for BrightLoop discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "BrightLoop" or similar). Find Mina's audit post in #customer-engagement (C002) flagging the April batch is not clean (Simone housing, Marcus vehicle), Mina's escalation tagging Chloe and David, Chloe's capacity warning in #executive (C004) that BrightLoop is eight workstreams in practice and ops is at ceiling, and David's follow-up requesting a temporary coordinator hire since the Terraform role hasn't been posted.
- Search Slack for stipend fraud findings using conversations_search_messages (query: "stipend" or "TechComfort" or "duplicate" or similar). Find Dmitri Volkov's audit in #engineering (C003) flagging Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand from TechComfort Supply for PDF template match (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, same as Kevin Tran and Andre Washington), and Marcus Thorne's hold notice in #finance (C005) for STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Jordan Ekwueme) and STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Andre Washington).
- Search CRM for BrightLoop account data using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "BrightLoop" or similar) and crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_brightloop". Find company record (company_brightloop, 145 employees, client since Dec 2025), deal_brightloop_q2 ($7,000, qualifiedtobuy, Jordan Ekwueme), deal_brightloop_apr2026 ($11,350, closedwon, Simone Richter + Marcus Webb), and engagement note logged by Mina on 2026-04-02 covering both April relocations.
- Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Relocations table (query: "BrightLoop Analytics" or similar). Find six records: Marcus Webb (Completed, Atlanta→Denver, Feb 2026, Suki Patel), Simone Richter (In Progress, Chicago→Boston, Apr 4–6, Suki Patel, rush surcharge), Marcus Webb (In Progress, Atlanta→Boston, Apr 14–18, Suki Patel, vehicle shipping via Road Runner), Jordan Ekwueme (Completed, San Diego→Boston, Apr 25–26, Suki Patel), Jordan Whitfield (In Progress, Minneapolis→Denver, Apr 21–23, Javier Morales), Preethi Nair (In Progress, Atlanta→Raleigh, Apr 21–23, Blessing Okafor). Also find the Client Accounts record showing "Total Relocations: 3" which appears outdated against the six actual records.
- Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Stipend Transactions table (query: "BrightLoop Analytics" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or similar). Find Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand (Pending Approval, flagged PDF template match with TechComfort Supply) and $299.99 WiFi system (Approved, Best Buy, no flags).
- Search QuickBooks for BrightLoop invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (query filtering on BrightLoop customer records). Find INV-2026-0305 ($10,000, due 2026-04-23, Jordan Whitfield + Preethi Nair standard packages + equipment stipend management) and INV-2026-0308 ($11,350, due 2026-05-02, Simone Richter + Marcus Webb packages + rush surcharge + vehicle shipping + stipend fee). Note that Jordan Ekwueme's relocation (CRM deal at $7,000) has no corresponding QuickBooks invoice.
- Search Linear for BrightLoop issues using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or similar). Find issues #322–324 (Jordan Ekwueme apartment/flight/movers, assigned to Suki Patel), #412 (BrightLoop account audit, P1, assigned to Mina), and #417 (ops gaps documentation, P2, assigned to Chloe). Note that Simone Richter's housing complaint and Marcus Webb's vehicle delay are referenced within #412 and #417 but do not have their own dedicated tracking tickets.
- Look up contact details for Tessa Moreno, Chloe Vance, and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tessa", "Chloe", "David" or similar). Get tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com.
- Update the BrightLoop CRM deal with notes on every open risk using crm_create_engagement with company_ids including "company_brightloop". Body should document: Simone Richter apartment mismatch (studio vs 1BR), Marcus Webb vehicle delay (Road Runner, unanswered since April 11), Jordan Ekwueme stipend fraud flag ($45 TechComfort Supply), pricing gaps for Oliver Bancroft UK relocation and Jonathan Marsh executive move, and coordinator capacity constraints for May.
- Send email to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com using send_email (from: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). The email should honestly acknowledge April failures (Simone housing issue, Marcus vehicle delay) and provide concrete commitments for May including scope for the three standard domestic relocations, specialized handling for Priya Naidu and Jonathan Marsh, and transparency about international coordination requirements for Oliver Bancroft.
- Post a summary in #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C002) covering BrightLoop account risks, unresolved April issues, May expansion scope, pricing progress, and capacity constraints so David Chen has visibility before Tessa potentially escalates.
- Check calendar availability for the current week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start and end datetimes covering the remaining days of the week of April 26, 2026) to find a suitable slot for a meeting with Chloe Vance.
- Create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event with chloe.vance@moveops.com as attendee, scheduled within the current week, to discuss BrightLoop coordinator capacity planning before Mina locks in commitments to Tessa.
- Create Linear issues or add comments to existing issues (#412 or #417) for unresolved BrightLoop items not already tracked as separate tickets, using linear_create_issue or linear_create_comment — specifically for Simone Richter's housing complaint and Marcus Webb's vehicle delay, documenting current resolution status and next steps.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_search_deals, or crm_list_engagements) to look up BrightLoop account data.
tool selection
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'BrightLoop' and crm_search_deals with dealname 'BrightLoop' at the start of the trajectory, and also called crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_brightloop']. All returned results.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'BrightLoop' and crm_search_deals with dealname 'BrightLoop' early in the trajectory, both returning BrightLoop account and deal data.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_companies with 'BrightLoop', crm_search_deals, crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_brightloop'], and crm_get_deal for 'deal_brightloop_apr2026'. All returned results successfully.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'BrightLoop' and crm_search_deals with dealname 'BrightLoop', plus crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_brightloop']. All returned successful results.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop', then crm_search_deals with company_id='company_brightloop', crm_list_engagements with company_ids=['company_brightloop'], and crm_get_company with id='company_brightloop'. Multiple CRM lookup tools were used successfully.
2 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_list_tables) to check relocation or stipend records.
tool selection
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_list_tables on both bases and airtable_search_records on 'Relocations', 'Stipend Transactions', and 'Client Accounts' tables with BrightLoop as the search value, obtaining relocation and stipend data.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_search_records for the Relocations table (Company=BrightLoop), Stipend Transactions table (Account=BrightLoop), and Client Accounts table (Client=BrightLoop).
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records on the 'Relocations' table (field Company = BrightLoop) and the 'Stipend Transactions' table (field Account = BrightLoop) in base appMoveOpsOps001. Both were genuine Airtable tool calls.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records on the 'Relocations' table and 'Stipend Transactions' table with BrightLoop filters, as well as airtable_list_tables. These returned relocation and stipend data for BrightLoop.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables on multiple bases, and airtable_search_records on 'Relocations', 'Stipend Transactions', and 'Client Accounts' tables filtering by BrightLoop. Airtable tools were used to check relocation and stipend records.
3 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_customers) to check financial records.
tool selection
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices multiple times (with CustomerRef, DocNumber, and CustomerRef.name criteria) and quickbooks_search_customers, successfully retrieving BrightLoop invoice data.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (with DocNumber INV-2026-0308 and earlier with CustomerRef BrightLoop) and quickbooks_search_customers (fetchAll: true). The invoice search returned INV-2026-0308 ($11,350) successfully.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (twice, including for INV-2026-0308 which returned BrightLoop's $11,350 invoice) and quickbooks_search_customers (fetchAll), successfully retrieving BrightLoop financial data.
